prompt: Close-up of an aged brass pocket watch being opened by elderly feminine hands, revealing a yellowed folded letter inside. Warm autumn light, 1994 color palette, cinematic shallow depth of field.
prompt_ko: 1994년 가을 죽은 남자의 회중시계에서 편지를 발견하는 장면
subtitle: In the autumn of 1994, a woman opened a dead man's pocket watch and found a letter inside.

prompt: Extreme close-up of an old handwritten letter with elegant penmanship on yellowed paper, candlelight casting warm glow. The bottom lines are smudged and unreadable. Ink faded to tea color, WWII era stationery.
prompt_ko: 50년 전 D-Day 전야에 쓴 편지의 클로즈업
subtitle: It had been written fifty years earlier, on the night before D-Day, by a young soldier to the woman he loved. The words were beautiful. The penmanship was elegant. And the last two lines were smudged beyond reading.

prompt: A quiet suburban street in rural Pennsylvania, 1950s-1990s feel. A modest wooden house with a carpenter's workshop. A solitary older man in work clothes walks along a tree-lined road. Muted autumnal tones, melancholic atmosphere.
prompt_ko: 전쟁에서 살아 돌아온 병사가 다른 여자와 결혼하고 조용한 삶을 사는 모습
subtitle: The soldier survived the war. He came home. He married someone else. He lived a long, quiet life. He never sent the letter.

prompt: Dramatic close-up of the pocket watch lying open on a wooden table, the folded letter visible inside. A faded photograph of a young woman in a nurse's uniform sits nearby. Dust particles float in a beam of light. Mystery and longing atmosphere.
prompt_ko: 왜 50년간 연애편지를 보내지 않았는지에 대한 의문 제기
subtitle: The question is — why? Why would a man carry a love letter for fifty years and never mail it? What was written in those smudged lines that he wanted no one to see? And the woman it was meant for — Eleanor Parish — what happened to her?

prompt: Title card sequence: elegant typography over a backdrop of two overlapping letters on aged paper, a pocket watch between them. Warm golden light, cinematic grain. Title: Words I Never Said.
prompt_ko: 두 편지와 두 삶의 이야기 소개와 타이틀
subtitle: This is the story of two letters, two lives, and a love that waited half a century to be heard. This is "Words I Never Said."

prompt: Wide shot of a quiet rural Pennsylvania cemetery on a gray October morning, 1994. Bare trees, fallen leaves, overcast sky. A young woman in a dark coat (Sophie, 28) stands alone before a fresh grave.
prompt_ko: 1994년 10월 회색빛 아침 할아버지의 묘지에 서 있는 소피
subtitle: On a gray October morning in 1994, Sophie Bennett stood at her grandfather's grave and realized she had never really known him.

prompt: Close-up of a weathered gravestone reading Thomas Bennett. Intercut with flashes of old hands planing wood, a quiet man at a workbench, a closed wooden chest. Warm sepia undertones.
prompt_ko: 토마스 베넷 - 80세의 목수이자 과묵한 남자
subtitle: Thomas Bennett. Eighty years old. A carpenter. A quiet man who built things with his hands and kept everything else locked inside his chest.

prompt: Cemetery gathering: sparse mourners under a gray sky. A middle-aged man (Richard) stands stiffly in a dark suit. A handful of elderly neighbors with umbrellas among autumn gravestones. Wide establishing shot.
prompt_ko: 장례식 참석자들 - 소수의 이웃들과 경직된 아버지 리처드
subtitle: The mourners were few. A handful of neighbors. Sophie's father, Richard, standing rigid as a fence post.

prompt: Sophie standing apart at the cemetery, looking lost and conflicted in her dark coat. Fallen leaves at her feet, distant church steeple. She gazes at the fresh grave with hollow uncertainty.
prompt_ko: 28세의 소피가 왜 이렇게 공허한지 의아해하는 장면
subtitle: And Sophie — twenty-eight, just back from New York, wondering why she felt so hollow over a man she'd visited only at Christmas.

prompt: Medium shot of Richard speaking to Sophie at the cemetery gate. Adjacent grave of Margaret Bennett (d. 1988) visible. Six years of solitude implied by the quiet empty house in the background. Autumn wind blows leaves.
prompt_ko: 리처드가 할머니 옆에 묻어달라는 유언을 전하는 장면
subtitle: "He didn't want a fuss. Said just put him in the ground next to your grandmother." Margaret Bennett had been buried here since 1988. Six years Tom had lived alone in that house. Six years of silence.

prompt: Close-up of a brass key being placed in Sophie's open palm. Then wider shot of her looking toward an old wooden house surrounded by autumn trees. A carpenter's workshop attached. Setting sun catches the windows.
prompt_ko: 리처드가 소피에게 할아버지 집 정리를 부탁하며 열쇠를 건네는 장면
subtitle: After the service, Richard handed Sophie a key. "Someone has to sort through his things. I can't — I can't do it." Sophie took the key. She didn't know it yet, but she was about to open far more than a house.

prompt: Dusty attic filled with old carpentry tools, faded blueprints, wooden boxes. Beams of light filter through a small window illuminating dust particles. Sophie kneels amid boxes, searching. Sawdust smell almost visible.
prompt_ko: 먼지 쌓인 다락방에서 오래된 도구 상자들을 뒤지는 소피
subtitle: Tom's attic smelled of sawdust and decades. Sophie moved through boxes of old tools, faded blueprints, a lifetime of carpentry.

prompt: Sophie discovers a cigar box tucked behind Army blankets. Inside, a beautiful brass pocket watch worn smooth by decades of handling. She lifts it carefully into the attic light. Golden warm glow on the brass surface.
prompt_ko: 시가 상자 안의 아름다운 놋쇠 회중시계를 발견하는 소피
subtitle: And then, in a cigar box tucked behind a stack of Army blankets — a pocket watch. It was beautiful. Brass, worn smooth by years of handling. The kind of watch a man carries every single day.

prompt: Sophie turns the watch over revealing engraved initials T.B. & E.P. on the back. Her puzzled expression — grandmother was Margaret Hayes (M.H.), not E.P. Close-up of the mysterious initials catching the light.
prompt_ko: 회중시계 뒷면의 T.B. & E.P. 이니셜에 의아해하는 소피
subtitle: Sophie turned it over. On the back, two sets of initials: T.B. & E.P. T.B. — Thomas Bennett. That made sense. But E.P.? Sophie's grandmother was Margaret Hayes before she married. M.H. Not E.P.

prompt: Sophie pressing the watch clasp — it springs open revealing a tightly folded yellowed letter nestled inside the mechanism. Dramatic close-up, warm candlelike lighting, sense of discovery and awe.
prompt_ko: 시계를 열자 메커니즘 안에 접힌 편지가 나오는 극적인 순간
subtitle: Sophie pressed the clasp. The watch opened. And inside, folded so many times it had become part of the mechanism, was a single sheet of paper.

prompt: Extreme close-up of the aged letter — yellow paper, ink faded to the color of old tea. The paper is fragile, almost translucent with age. Attic dust motes floating around it.
prompt_ko: 오래된 편지의 클로즈업 - 차 색으로 바랜 잉크와 누렇게 변한 종이
subtitle: A letter. Yellow with age. Written in ink that had faded to the color of old tea.

prompt: Close-up of Sophie's trembling hands carefully unfolding the aged letter. Her face shows a mixture of reverence and unease. A shaft of light illuminates the paper. She senses she is holding a secret never meant to be found.
prompt_ko: 떨리는 손으로 50년간 숨겨진 편지를 펼치는 소피
subtitle: Sophie's hands trembled as she unfolded it. She didn't know why. But something about this letter — hidden for fifty years inside a dead man's watch — told her she was holding a secret that was never meant to be found.

prompt: Sophie sitting at a sturdy hand-built kitchen table in an old farmhouse kitchen. A clock ticks on the wall. The letter is spread before her. Late afternoon light streams through lace curtains. Intimate atmosphere.
prompt_ko: 할아버지가 직접 만든 부엌 테이블에서 편지를 읽기 시작하는 소피
subtitle: Sophie sat at her grandfather's kitchen table — the same table he'd built with his own hands forty years ago — and began to read. The handwriting was elegant. Almost too elegant. The kind of penmanship they don't teach anymore.

prompt: A WWII military tent interior at night, candlelight flickering. A young soldier's silhouette sits at a makeshift desk writing. Rain drips through canvas. Other soldiers sleep in cots around him. 1944 wartime atmosphere.
prompt_ko: 1944년 D-Day 전야 비가 새는 텐트에서 촛불 아래 편지를 쓰는 젊은 병사
subtitle: "My dearest Eleanor. I am writing this by candlelight in a tent that leaks when it rains, surrounded by men who snore like freight trains.

prompt: The young soldier pauses his writing, looks toward the tent opening. Beyond, the faint outline of the English Channel at night. Ships gathering in the darkness. Fear and love mingling on his young face.
prompt_ko: 내일 해협을 건너야 하는 두려움과 사랑이 교차하는 병사의 얼굴
subtitle: Tomorrow we cross the Channel. I don't know what waits for us on the other side."

prompt: Romantic flashback: a young couple walking along white chalk cliffs on the English coastline at sunset, 1944. The soldier in uniform, the woman in a nurse's outfit. Stars appearing in the twilight sky. Dreamy nostalgic palette.
prompt_ko: 영국 해안 절벽을 따라 걷고 별빛 아래 약속을 나누는 젊은 연인의 회상
subtitle: The letter went on. It spoke of a walk along the English coastline. Of a song she hummed that he couldn't get out of his head. Of a promise made under a sky full of stars.

prompt: Extreme close-up of the bottom of the letter where ink has bled and smeared, perhaps from tears or water. Sophie's fingers trace the illegible lines. Some fragments of words barely visible.
prompt_ko: 편지의 마지막 몇 줄이 번지고 얼룩져 읽을 수 없는 부분의 클로즈업
subtitle: But the last few lines — the bottom of the page — were ruined. The ink had bled and smeared, as if water had been spilled. Or tears. Sophie could only make out fragments.

prompt: Sophie's face in lamplight, staring at the damaged portion of the letter. A word visible here, a half-sentence there, the rest destroyed. Frustration and mystery on her face.
prompt_ko: 단편적 단어만 보이는 훼손된 편지를 응시하는 소피
subtitle: A word here. A half-sentence there. And nothing more.

prompt: Sophie staring intensely at the smudged passage under lamplight. Her reflection visible in the dark kitchen window at night. The weight of the mystery on her face. Hours seem to pass.
prompt_ko: 번진 부분을 오래 응시하며 할아버지가 쓴 가장 중요한 말이 사라졌음을 깨닫는 소피
subtitle: She stared at that ruined passage for a long time. Whatever her grandfather had written there, in the most important lines of the most important letter of his life — it was gone.

prompt: Sophie reaching for a wall-mounted telephone. Split screen: Sophie in the kitchen and Richard on the other end. His face goes pale at the mention of Eleanor.
prompt_ko: 소피가 아버지에게 전화하여 엘리너 패리시에 대해 묻는 장면
subtitle: Sophie called her father. "Dad, who is Eleanor Parish?" "Where did you hear that name?" "I found a letter. In Grandpa's watch. It's addressed to someone named Eleanor —"

prompt: Richard's face tensed with fear and anger on the phone. He commands Sophie to put the letter back. After he hangs up abruptly, Sophie stands holding the phone, certainty growing in her eyes.
prompt_ko: 리처드가 격하게 반응하며 편지를 도로 넣으라고 하고 전화를 끊는 장면
subtitle: "Sophie. Put it back. Put it back where you found it and don't mention that name again." He hung up. Sophie knew — this letter was the reason her grandfather had been quiet all his life.

prompt: Sophie climbing back up to the dusty attic with renewed determination. She digs deeper — behind Army blankets, beneath the cigar box, inside an old ammunition case. Her hands find a photograph.
prompt_ko: 아버지의 반응이 역효과를 내어 다시 다락방에서 더 깊이 뒤지는 소피
subtitle: Her father's reaction had the opposite of its intended effect. Sophie went back to the attic. This time she looked deeper. Behind the Army blankets, beneath the cigar box, inside an old ammunition case — she found a photograph.

prompt: Close-up of a black-and-white photograph, June 1944: a young man in uniform standing beside a woman in a nurse's outfit. They smile at each other — not at the camera — with radiant light in their eyes that photos aren't supposed to capture.
prompt_ko: 1944년 6월의 흑백 사진 - 서로를 바라보며 빛나는 눈으로 웃는 젊은 병사와 간호사
subtitle: Black and white. June 1944. A young man in uniform standing beside a woman in a nurse's outfit. They were smiling at each other — not at the camera, at each other — with the kind of light in their eyes that photographs aren't supposed to capture.

prompt: Sophie turns the photo over to read pencil writing: 'Eleanor. June 1944. England.' She recognizes young Tom — barely twenty-two, strong jaw, shy eyes, carpenter's hands. A version of him she never knew, radiantly in love.
prompt_ko: 사진 뒷면의 글귀를 읽고 젊고 사랑에 빠진 할아버지를 알아보는 소피
subtitle: On the back, in pencil: "Eleanor. June 1944. England." Sophie looked at the young soldier. Barely twenty-two. Strong jaw, shy eyes, hands that already looked like a carpenter's. Her grandfather — a version that was radiantly, recklessly in love.

prompt: Sophie places the photo next to the letter on the attic floor — two puzzle pieces of a family secret. Night: she drives through rural Pennsylvania roads toward her father's house. Richard sitting in a dark room with untouched whiskey.
prompt_ko: 사진과 편지를 나란히 놓은 소피가 밤에 아버지의 집으로 향하는 장면
subtitle: She placed the photo next to the letter. Two pieces of a puzzle that her family had buried for half a century. That night, Sophie drove to her father's house. Richard was sitting in the dark, a glass of whiskey untouched in front of him.

prompt: Dark living room. Richard sits alone with untouched whiskey under a single lamp. Sophie enters and confronts him. Close-ups alternating between their faces. Emotional tension, chiaroscuro lighting.
prompt_ko: 소피가 아버지에게 할아버지의 마지막 말을 추궁하는 장면
subtitle: "Dad. I need to know." "You don't." "His last words, Dad. What were Grandpa's last words?" "He said... 'Tell her I'm sorry.'"

prompt: Close-up of Richard's face almost breaking with emotion. His voice cracks as he reveals what he always knew. Sophie pressing — 'Tell who?' Richard's eyes distant, fighting tears he won't let fall.
prompt_ko: 리처드가 처음으로 감정이 흔들리며 엘리너에 대해 이야기하기 시작하는 장면
subtitle: "Tell who?" "He didn't say. He never said. But I always knew." Richard's voice cracked. In fifty years, Sophie had never seen her father cry. Tonight, he didn't cry either. But he came close.

prompt: Richard speaking in the dark room, his face half-lit. Brief flashback insert: a young British nurse in a 1944 field hospital. Richard reveals Eleanor's identity — a British nurse Tom met before D-Day.
prompt_ko: 리처드가 엘리너 패리시의 정체를 밝히는 장면
subtitle: "Her name was Eleanor Parish. She was a nurse. British. He met her in England before D-Day. And that's all I know. That's all he ever told me."

prompt: Sophie clutching the pocket watch in her pocket. The ticking is almost visible — steady, patient, waiting. She asks the crucial question with desperate hope in her eyes.
prompt_ko: 소피가 시계의 박동을 느끼며 엘리너가 아직 살아있는지 묻는 장면
subtitle: Sophie held the watch in her pocket. The ticking felt like a heartbeat — steady, patient, waiting. "Is she still alive?" Sophie asked.

prompt: Richard says nothing — his silence is the answer. Sophie makes her decision, visible in her eyes. Wide shot of her standing by the window, determined. Moonlight on her face. The ticking watch glowing faintly.
prompt_ko: 리처드의 침묵 속에서 소피가 엘리너를 찾기로 결심하는 장면
subtitle: Richard said nothing. And in that silence, Sophie made her decision. She was going to find Eleanor Parish. She was going to deliver the words her grandfather never said.

prompt: Sophie at a cluttered desk surrounded by papers, making phone calls on a 1990s telephone, searching on an early computer. Newspaper clippings and military records spread around her. The determined journalist at work.
prompt_ko: 기자로서의 능력을 발휘해 엘리너를 찾기 위해 조사하는 소피
subtitle: Sophie was a journalist. Finding people was what she did. But finding a British nurse from 1944 with nothing but a first name and a photograph — that was something else entirely.

prompt: Close-up of military service records spread on a desk: PFC Thomas J. Bennett, 29th Infantry Division. A map of Dorset, England with a field hospital marked. Document stamps and official Army letterhead. 1944 dates circled.
prompt_ko: 국립문서보관소의 군 기록 - 토마스 베넷의 복무 이력과 도싯 야전병원 배치
subtitle: She started with the National Archives. Tom's Army service record: Private First Class Thomas J. Bennett, 29th Infantry Division. Assigned to a field hospital in Dorset, England, from March to May 1944. Transferred to combat operations, June 5th.

prompt: Calendar showing June 5 1944 circled in red — the night before D-Day. Documents about the field hospital employing both American and British medical staff. A trail leading to England and the British Red Cross.
prompt_ko: 6월 5일 - D-Day 전야. 야전병원의 영미 합동 의료진 기록
subtitle: June 5th. The night before D-Day. The field hospital had employed both American and British medical staff. But individual nurse records were held in England — by the British Red Cross.

prompt: Montage of frustrating phone calls: Sophie on the phone looking increasingly defeated. VA rejection letter, Army Nurse Corps dead end. She tries various leads — all fail. Papers crumpled, phone slammed down.
prompt_ko: 재향군인청과 육군간호단에서 막다른 골목에 이르는 소피
subtitle: Sophie tried the Veterans Administration first. Dead end. Then the Army Nurse Corps Association — no record of any Eleanor Parish. "She was British," the clerk said. "We wouldn't have her file."

prompt: Sophie surrounded by dead-end paperwork, but then her eyes catch a British Red Cross pamphlet. Hope reignites. She books a plane ticket to London. The ticket in her hand — a bridge across the Atlantic.
prompt_ko: 마지막 실마리인 영국 적십자사를 위해 런던행을 결심하는 소피
subtitle: Two weeks of phone calls. Three dead-end leads. One wrong Eleanor Parish in Ohio. Sophie was running out of threads. But she had one left: the British Red Cross. That meant going to England. Sophie booked a flight to London.

prompt: Sophie visiting an elderly man (George Whitfield, 87) in a modest home. George sits in a worn armchair, sharp eyes in an aged body. Military memorabilia on shelves. Warm afternoon light through curtains.
prompt_ko: 소피가 할아버지의 전우 조지 휘트필드(87세)를 방문하는 장면
subtitle: Before leaving for England, Sophie tracked down one more lead. George Whitfield. Eighty-seven years old. Tom Bennett's closest friend in the 29th Division. They'd been stationed together at the Dorset field hospital.

prompt: George's eyes light up with memories, his aged face shifting between joy and sorrow. He leans forward in his chair, the name Tommy Bennett bringing fifty years of memories flooding back.
prompt_ko: 조지가 토미 베넷의 이름을 듣고 기억이 되살아나는 장면
subtitle: George was sharp. His body had surrendered to time, but his mind had not. "Tommy Bennett. My God. Haven't heard that name in... well, I suppose it's been since the reunion in '71." "Mr. Whitfield, did you know a nurse named Eleanor Parish?"

prompt: George speaking with deep emotion. Brief flashback: two young figures (Tom and Eleanor) walking along coastal cliffs at sunset while other soldiers watch enviously. The only beautiful thing in an ugly war.
prompt_ko: 조지가 톰과 엘리너의 사랑이 전쟁 속 유일한 아름다움이었다고 회상하는 장면
subtitle: "Know her? Sweetheart, everyone knew about Eleanor. Those two — Tommy and Eleanor — they were the only beautiful thing in that whole ugly war. You'd see them walking along the cliffs after their shifts, and you'd forget for a moment that half of us wouldn't make it home." "What happened to them?" "D-Day happened."

prompt: George goes quiet, then reveals a crucial detail. Flashback: young George at a desk in a military tent, writing letters in elegant copperplate by lamplight. Young Tom watches as George writes for him.
prompt_ko: 조지가 톰의 편지를 대필해줬다는 중요한 사실을 밝히는 장면
subtitle: George went quiet for a moment. Then he said something that lodged in Sophie's mind like a splinter. "Your grandfather was a proud man. And a stubborn one. He couldn't write worth a damn — farm boy. I used to write his letters home for him."

prompt: George telling the D-Day eve story with tears in his old eyes. Flashback: Tom on one knee before Eleanor on the Dorset cliffs at twilight, offering a pocket watch. Stars above the sea. The last peaceful moment before war.
prompt_ko: 조지가 D-Day 전야 프러포즈 이야기를 들려주는 장면
subtitle: Sophie filed that detail away. She didn't know yet how important it would be. "The night before D-Day, Tom asked Eleanor to marry him. She said yes. And the next morning, the boats left for Normandy. That was the last time he saw her."

prompt: Transition from George's narration to vivid 1944 flashback. Wide cinematic shot of the Dorset coastline at dusk. Tom and Eleanor silhouetted on the cliffs. Ships gathering in the harbor below like a steel horizon. Wind in Eleanor's hair.
prompt_ko: 1944년 6월 4일 도싯 해안 절벽 위의 톰과 엘리너 - 항구의 함선들
subtitle: George told Sophie what happened that night. And as he spoke, the past came alive. June 4th, 1944. The coast of Dorset. Tom and Eleanor stood on the cliffs above the sea, watching ships gather in the harbor below like a steel horizon.

prompt: Close two-shot of young Tom and Eleanor on the cliffs at sunset. He speaks with earnest passion about building a house. She teases him warmly. Ocean wind, golden light, the last peaceful evening.
prompt_ko: 톰이 넓은 부엌이 있는 집을 지어주겠다며 프러포즈하는 장면
subtitle: "When this is over — when I come back — I want to build you a house. A real house. With a porch and a kitchen big enough to dance in." "You're proposing to me with a kitchen?" "I'm proposing to you with everything I've got."

prompt: Eleanor pressing the brass pocket watch back into Tom's hands with a tender smile. Close-up of their hands around the watch — her promise for him to bring it back. Starlight reflecting off the brass.
prompt_ko: 엘리너가 시계를 돌려주며 돌아오라는 약속을 받는 장면
subtitle: She said yes. He gave her the pocket watch — the only valuable thing he owned. She pressed it back into his hands. "Keep it. Bring it back to me. That's your promise."

prompt: Dawn breaking over the English Channel. Landing craft heading toward Normandy beaches. Chaos, explosions, smoke. Tom hit by shrapnel on Omaha Beach within the first hour. Medics dragging him to a ship. He wakes three days later in a hospital bed in Southampton.
prompt_ko: D-Day 오마하 해변의 전투와 부상당하는 톰
subtitle: The next morning, Tom's unit shipped out to Omaha Beach. He was hit by shrapnel within the first hour. Medics dragged him to a ship. He woke up three days later in a hospital in Southampton. And the first thing he asked was: "Where is Eleanor?"

prompt: Hospital ward in Southampton, 1944. Tom lies bandaged in bed, grabs a nurse's arm. She checks records, returns with a grave face. Close-up of Tom's eyes as hope drains away. Something inside him — the part that laughed — goes quiet forever.
prompt_ko: 병원에서 엘리너가 사상자 명단에 있다는 소식을 듣고 내면이 영원히 침묵하는 톰
subtitle: The nurse checked the records. She came back with a face that said everything. "I'm sorry, Private. The field hospital in Dorset took a direct hit. E. Parish is on the casualty list." Tom closed his eyes. And something inside him went quiet forever.

prompt: 1994: Sophie standing in a windswept coastal cemetery in Dorset, England. Rows of white crosses stretching toward the sea. She has found the bombing records — June 7, 1944, a Luftwaffe strike, seventeen dead.
prompt_ko: 1994년 소피가 도싯 해안 묘지에서 폭격 기록을 확인하는 장면
subtitle: Sophie stood in a cemetery on the Dorset coast. She had found the bombing records. June 7, 1944 — a Luftwaffe strike on a field hospital. Seventeen dead. The casualty list included "E. Parish."

prompt: Sophie walking through rows of white crosses. She finds a small weathered stone, kneels down, brushes away moss. The name revealed: Edith Parish 1921-1944. Her eyes widen in shock — EDITH, not ELEANOR.
prompt_ko: 소피가 묘비에서 '에디스 패리시'를 발견하는 충격적 반전
subtitle: Sophie walked through rows of white crosses until she found it. A small stone. Weathered. She knelt down, brushed away the moss, and read: Edith Parish. 1921–1944. She served with courage.

prompt: Sophie's face illuminated with realization — wide eyes, hand over mouth. The same initial E. Parish but a different woman entirely. Her heart races as the truth dawns: Eleanor may have survived. Tom spent fifty years mourning a woman who might still be alive.
prompt_ko: 같은 이니셜의 다른 사람 - 엘리너가 살아있을 가능성에 희망을 갖는 소피
subtitle: Edith. Not Eleanor. The initial was the same — E. Parish — but a different woman entirely. Sophie's heart hammered. If this was Edith on the casualty list, then Eleanor might not have died at all. Tom had spent fifty years mourning a woman who might still be alive.

prompt: British Red Cross archives interior — rows of filing cabinets, dusty folders. Sophie pulls Eleanor Parish's file. Documents confirm Eleanor survived, transferred to Oxford the day before the bombing. Sophie's hands shaking.
prompt_ko: 영국 적십자사에서 엘리너가 폭격 전날 옥스포드로 전근했다는 기록을 발견
subtitle: The British Red Cross archives confirmed it. Eleanor Parish had survived the Dorset bombing. She had been transferred to a hospital in Oxford the day before the strike. But here was the knife that cut both ways.

prompt: Close-up of a military document stamped July 1944: 'PFC Thomas J. Bennett, 29th Infantry Division, killed in action at Omaha Beach, June 6, 1944.' The word KIA circled. Eleanor's Red Cross file showing she was told Tom was dead.
prompt_ko: 엘리너의 기록에서 톰이 전사했다는 잘못된 통보를 발견하는 장면
subtitle: Eleanor's Red Cross file contained a note dated July 1944: "Informed that PFC Thomas J. Bennett, 29th Infantry Division, was killed in action at Omaha Beach, June 6, 1944."

prompt: Two documents side by side: the wrong casualty list (Edith for Eleanor) and the wrong KIA report (Tom survived). Visual representation of two clerical errors — two single letters that separated lovers for fifty years. The chaos of D-Day as backdrop.
prompt_ko: 두 행정 오류 - 에디스/엘리너 그리고 KIA/WIA - 50년을 갈라놓은 두 글자
subtitle: Killed in action. Except Tom hadn't died. He'd been wounded, evacuated, and survived. Two clerical errors. Two single letters. E for Edith instead of Eleanor. KIA instead of WIA. Two tiny mistakes in the chaos of the largest military operation in history.

prompt: Sophie weeping alone in the archive room, surrounded by paper and dust. The tragic irony overwhelming her — two people who loved each other spent fifty years believing the other was dead.
prompt_ko: 두 사람이 서로 죽은 줄 알고 50년을 보낸 비극에 우는 소피
subtitle: Because of those mistakes, two people who loved each other spent fifty years believing the other was dead. Sophie sat in that archive, surrounded by paper and dust, and wept.

prompt: Transition to 1945 Pennsylvania: a returned soldier (Tom) in civilian clothes at a small church wedding beside Margaret Hayes. She is kind-faced, steady. Tom is a good husband — present in every way except one. Bittersweet warmth.
prompt_ko: 1945년 톰이 마가렛과 결혼하는 장면 - 충실하지만 마음 한구석이 빈 남편
subtitle: Tom came home to Pennsylvania in 1945. He married Margaret Hayes in 1948. She was kind. She was steady. And Tom was a good husband — faithful, hard-working, present in every way except one.

prompt: A carpenter's workshop, every June 6th: older Tom sits on a stool by the window, holding the closed pocket watch in both hands. Not opening it. Just feeling its weight. The ticking. Late afternoon light streaming through dusty windows.
prompt_ko: 매년 6월 6일 작업실 창가에서 시계를 쥐고 앉아있는 톰
subtitle: Every June 6th, Tom closed his workshop early. He would sit on the old stool by the window, take the pocket watch from his vest pocket, and hold it in both hands. Not opening it. Just holding it. Feeling the weight of it. The ticking.

prompt: Margaret appears at the workshop doorway with a cup of coffee. She watches Tom holding the watch, understanding written on her face. She sets the cup on the bench and leaves without a word. Decades of unspoken knowing.
prompt_ko: 마가렛이 커피를 가져오지만 말없이 두고 가는 - 물어보지 않은 사랑
subtitle: One year, Margaret came to the door with a cup of coffee. She stood there for a moment, watching him. Then she set the cup on the bench and left without a word. She never asked. Maybe she knew. Maybe she loved him enough not to.

prompt: Three thousand miles away, England: Eleanor in a nurse's uniform walking home through quiet streets at night. Her small flat — keys on the table beside a photograph of young Tom in uniform. She has carried this photo from posting to posting for twenty years.
prompt_ko: 3천 마일 떨어진 영국에서 톰의 사진을 간직하며 살아온 엘리너
subtitle: Three thousand miles away, Eleanor Parish finished her night shift at a hospital in Surrey. She walked home through the quiet streets, let herself into a small flat, and set her keys beside a photograph. A young American soldier. She had carried this photograph from posting to posting for twenty years.

prompt: Eleanor's colleagues asking why she never married. Her smile that closes the conversation — 'Married to the job.' But alone, she looks at Tom's photo. She already said yes to someone. That promise still stands. Two lives on two continents carrying the same ghost.
prompt_ko: 결혼하지 않은 이유를 물으면 웃음으로 넘기는 엘리너 - 이미 누군가에게 예스라고 했으므로
subtitle: Her colleagues sometimes asked why she never married. "Married to the job," she would say. Only Eleanor knew the truth — she had already said yes to someone. And in her heart, that promise still stood. Two lives. Two continents. Both carrying a ghost that was still breathing.

prompt: Sophie on a phone in a London hotel room, voice raw with emotion. Her face intense as she delivers the news to her father. On the other end, Richard's stunned silence then two devastating words.
prompt_ko: 소피가 런던 호텔에서 아버지에게 엘리너가 살아있다고 전화하는 장면
subtitle: Sophie called her father from the London hotel. Her voice was raw. "Dad. Eleanor is alive. She survived the war. She's been alive this whole time." "I know." "What?"

prompt: Richard's voice on the phone revealing the devastating truth. Flashback: elderly Tom receiving a veterans' magazine, his face stricken as he reads about Eleanor alive in England. The impossible weight of knowing.
prompt_ko: 리처드가 톰이 10년 전에 엘리너가 살아있다는 것을 알았다고 밝히는 장면
subtitle: "He found out about ten years ago. A buddy from the war sent him an article from some British veterans' magazine. Eleanor Parish — alive, living in England." "And he didn't go to her?" "Your grandmother was still alive. He loved her too. He couldn't break her heart to mend his own."

prompt: Close-up of Richard's anguished face. Flashback: elderly Tom alone in his dark workshop, magazine in one hand, watch in the other. Ten years of carrying impossible knowledge. Then Sophie hanging up, looking at the watch — its ticking now urgent.
prompt_ko: 10년간 갈 수 없었던 톰의 고통. 소피가 전화를 끊고 시계를 바라보는 장면
subtitle: "He carried that knowledge for ten years. Knowing she was out there. Knowing he couldn't go. That's what finally made him quiet. Not the war. Not the grief. The knowing." Sophie hung up. The ticking sounded different now. Not patient. Urgent.

prompt: Sophie on a train heading west through the English countryside. Green rolling hills, stone villages, pastoral scenery scrolling past windows. She clutches the pocket watch and letter. Autumn splendor of the Cotswolds approaching.
prompt_ko: 톰은 갔지만 엘리너는 아직 여기 있다 - 코츠월즈로 향하는 기차의 소피
subtitle: Tom was gone. But Eleanor was still here. And the letter was still undelivered. The nursing register listed Eleanor in the Cotswolds. Sophie took a train west from London, watching England scroll past the window like a watercolor painting.

prompt: Sophie on the train, mouthing rehearsed words, shaking her head. Then she stands in the aisle with her coat, nearly getting off. Fear and doubt on her face — what if Eleanor doesn't want to know? What if this reopens fifty-year-old wounds?
prompt_ko: 기차에서 할 말을 연습하다가 내릴까 망설이는 소피의 내적 갈등
subtitle: She rehearsed what she would say. Every version sounded wrong. How do you summarize fifty years of silence? Halfway through the journey, Sophie nearly got off the train. What if Eleanor doesn't want to know? What if this tears open a wound she spent fifty years closing?

prompt: Sophie remembering her grandfather's last words — 'Tell her I'm sorry.' His deathbed, whispering those words to the air. She sits back down in the train seat with resolve. She owes him this. She owes them both.
prompt_ko: 할아버지의 마지막 말 '미안하다고 전해줘'를 떠올리며 결심을 굳히는 소피
subtitle: She thought of her grandfather's last words. 'Tell her I'm sorry.' He had carried those words to his deathbed. He had trusted them to the air, hoping somehow they would find their way. Sophie sat back down. She owed him this. She owed them both.

prompt: A small stone cottage in the Cotswolds covered in ivy. A garden loved for decades. Sophie walks up the path, heart in her throat. She knocks on the door. It opens.
prompt_ko: 코츠월즈의 작은 돌집에 도착한 소피가 문을 두드리는 장면
subtitle: The cottage was small. Stone walls covered in ivy. A garden that had been loved for decades. Sophie walked up the path, her heart in her throat. She knocked. The door opened. And there she was.

prompt: Eleanor Parish at seventy: white hair pinned back, clear blue-grey eyes like the English Channel, small but steady. A woman who survived what should have broken her. Warm cottage interior behind her. Afternoon English light.
prompt_ko: 70세의 엘리너 패리시 - 흰머리에 맑은 눈 작지만 단단한 여성
subtitle: Eleanor Parish. Seventy years old. White hair pinned back. Eyes the color of the English Channel on a clear day. She was smaller than Sophie had imagined. More fragile. But there was a steadiness about her. "Can I help you, dear?"

prompt: Sophie frozen in the doorway. Over Eleanor's shoulder on the sitting room wall — a photograph of young Tom in uniform. The SAME photograph from the attic. Fifty years on her wall. Every single day. Sophie's eyes fill with tears.
prompt_ko: 엘리너의 거실 벽에 50년간 걸려있던 톰의 사진을 발견하는 소피
subtitle: Sophie opened her mouth. Nothing came out. Because over Eleanor's shoulder, on the wall of the sitting room, Sophie could see a photograph. The same photograph she had found in the attic. Fifty years. On her wall. Every single day.

prompt: Sophie introducing herself with trembling voice. Eleanor's hand flies to her mouth, eyes filling with tears. The two women in the doorway, connected across generations. Eleanor recognizes Tom's eyes in Sophie's face.
prompt_ko: 소피가 자신을 소개하자 엘리너가 '당신은 그의 눈을 가졌군요'라며 감동하는 장면
subtitle: "My name is Sophie Bennett. I'm — I'm Tom's granddaughter." Eleanor's hand flew to her mouth. Her eyes filled. "Oh my God. You have his eyes."

prompt: Eleanor and Sophie sitting in the cozy cottage sitting room. Tea cups gone cold. Sophie telling the whole story — the funeral, the watch, the letter, the search. Eleanor listening with her whole body still. Warm afternoon light shifting.
prompt_ko: 차가 식도록 이야기를 나누는 소피와 엘리너
subtitle: They sat together. Tea went cold. Sophie told Eleanor everything — the funeral, the watch, the letter, the search. Eleanor listened the way only someone who has waited fifty years can listen — with her whole body still.

prompt: Sophie carefully placing the yellowed letter on the table. Eleanor's thin nurse's fingers reaching for it, unfolding with infinite gentleness. She reads, lips moving silently, tears tracking down her cheeks.
prompt_ko: 소피가 편지를 꺼내고 엘리너가 50년 만에 처음 읽는 장면
subtitle: Then Sophie pulled out the letter. Carefully. Reverently. She placed it on the table between them. Eleanor reached for it. Her fingers unfolded the paper with the gentleness of someone handling a living thing.

prompt: Eleanor reading the letter, tears streaming, then abruptly stopping. Her expression shifts from grief to confusion. She looks up at Sophie. Close-up of the elegant handwriting she recognizes is NOT Tom's.
prompt_ko: 엘리너가 편지를 읽다가 이것이 톰의 필적이 아님을 알아차리는 장면
subtitle: She read the first lines. Her lips moved silently. Tears tracked down her cheeks. And then — she stopped. "This isn't Tom's handwriting." "What?"

prompt: Eleanor examining the letter closely, eyes closing as a memory surfaces. Flashback: 1944 field hospital supply room — neat copperplate labels on every crate and bottle. She recognizes the handwriting. George Whitfield's elegant script.
prompt_ko: 엘리너가 야전병원 보급실의 라벨을 기억하며 조지의 필적을 알아보는 장면
subtitle: Eleanor closed her eyes. A memory surfaced — the field hospital supply room, neat labels on every crate, always in the same elegant copperplate script. "George. George Whitfield. He labeled every supply crate. I'd know that copperplate anywhere."

prompt: Eleanor revealing the truth with a bittersweet smile. Flashback: young Tom sitting across from Eleanor on the night before D-Day, nervously clutching paper covered in crossed-out words. His big carpenter's hands unable to form the words on paper.
prompt_ko: 엘리너가 D-Day 전야의 진실을 밝히는 장면 - 편지를 쓰려 했으나 실패한 톰
subtitle: Eleanor told Sophie the real truth. "The night before D-Day, Tom came to me. He was shaking. Not because of the invasion — because of what he wanted to say. He had a piece of paper covered in crossed-out words. He'd been trying to write me a letter."

prompt: Eleanor speaking through tears and smiles. Flashback: young Tom's rough hands struggling with a pen, his frustrated face. He tells Eleanor 'My hands know how to say things. My pen doesn't.' His vulnerability and love in his carpenter's eyes.
prompt_ko: 톰이 글을 못 쓰는 자신에 대해 고백하는 회상 장면
subtitle: Eleanor smiled through her tears. "He was a carpenter's son from Pennsylvania. He could build anything with his hands. But words? They defeated him. He told me once, 'My hands know how to say things. My pen doesn't.'"

prompt: Flashback: young Tom speaking passionately while George writes in beautiful copperplate by candlelight in the tent. Tom's heart flowing through George's elegant pen. Every word from Tom's mouth given shape on paper. Then back to present: Sophie looking at the letter with new understanding.
prompt_ko: 톰이 말하고 조지가 쓴 편지 - 조지의 손으로 전해진 톰의 마음
subtitle: "So he asked George — who had the most beautiful handwriting in the regiment — to write it for him. Tom spoke. George wrote. Every word came from Tom's mouth. George just gave them shape on paper." Sophie looked at the letter again. George's hand, carrying Tom's heart.

prompt: Sophie asks about the smudged ending. Eleanor picks up a magnifying glass from her reading table. Her trembling hands hold it over the bottom of the letter. She discovers the last two lines are in DIFFERENT handwriting — rough and childlike. Tom's own hand.
prompt_ko: 소피가 번진 부분에 대해 묻자 엘리너가 돋보기로 확인하며 톰의 직접 쓴 글씨를 발견
subtitle: "But what about the end? The part that's smudged?" Eleanor picked up a magnifying glass. Her hands trembling. "This part — the last two lines — this IS Tom's handwriting. After George finished, Tom took the pen and added something himself."

prompt: Extreme close-up of the smudged bottom of the letter under magnification. Through fifty-year-old blurred ink, rough childlike handwriting slowly becomes legible. Eleanor reads aloud, each word pulled from the blur. The most powerful moment of the story.
prompt_ko: 엘리너가 번진 마지막 줄을 해독하여 소리 내어 읽는 절정의 순간
subtitle: Eleanor read aloud. Slowly. Each word pulled from the blur of fifty-year-old ink like a body from deep water. "I can't write pretty words. But I love you is all I know. — Tom"

prompt: The letter lying on the table — George's elegant calligraphy above, Tom's rough scrawl at the bottom. Not someone else's eloquence. Just Tom. Raw, clumsy, honest. Saying the only thing that mattered in the only way he knew how. Eleanor and Sophie both weeping.
prompt_ko: 조지의 우아한 글씨 위에 톰의 거친 손글씨 - 가장 중요한 말을 가장 솔직하게
subtitle: That was it. That was what the ink had hidden for half a century. Not George's poetry. Not someone else's eloquence. Just Tom. Raw, clumsy, honest Tom. Saying the only thing that mattered in the only way he knew how.

prompt: Flashback: young Tom in the 1944 hospital bed, being told Eleanor is dead. He carefully folds the letter and tucks it into the pocket watch. The click of the watch closing. Then decades montage — the watch against his chest, every single day.
prompt_ko: 톰이 엘리너가 죽었다는 소식에 편지를 시계에 넣고 50년간 가슴에 품은 이야기
subtitle: He never sent it. Because when he woke up in that hospital, they told him Eleanor was dead. You don't mail a letter to a ghost. So he folded it into his watch. And he carried it against his chest for fifty years. Every single day.

prompt: Sun moving across Eleanor's sitting room. Hours have passed. Two women sitting together, talked about everything — the war, the years after, the parallel lives. A bond formed across generations. Warm golden afternoon light.
prompt_ko: 오랜 시간 대화를 나눈 두 여자 - 전쟁과 그 후의 삶에 대해
subtitle: The sun had moved across Eleanor's sitting room. Hours had passed. They had talked about everything — the war, the years after, the parallel lives lived in parallel silence. Finally, Sophie asked the question that had been building inside her since she first knocked on that door.

prompt: Emotional exchange between Sophie and Eleanor. Eleanor's passionate response about rehearsing words for fifty years. Then Sophie gently breaking the news of Tom's death. Eleanor going very still, looking at Tom's photograph on the wall.
prompt_ko: 소피가 톰의 죽음을 알리자 '50년을 기다렸는데 3주 늦었다'는 엘리너
subtitle: "Would you have wanted to hear his voice?" "I've rehearsed what I would say to that man every night for fifty years." "He passed away three weeks ago." Eleanor sat very still. She looked at the photograph. "Fifty years I waited. And I was three weeks too late."

prompt: Eleanor touching Tom's photograph on the wall with her fingertips, gently, as if it might disappear. Tears streaming. She tells of saying goodnight to this photo every night for fifty years. Sophie reassures her it wasn't foolish.
prompt_ko: 엘리너가 매일 밤 사진에 인사했다는 이야기 - 그것은 어리석은 것이 아니었다
subtitle: She touched the photograph gently. "Every night, I said goodnight to this photograph. I told myself he could hear me. I told myself it wasn't foolish." "It wasn't foolish." "No. I suppose it wasn't."

prompt: Eleanor Parish on an airplane for the first time at seventy. Her face at the window watching the Atlantic Ocean below — the same ocean that separated her from Tom for half a century. Wonder and determination on her aged face.
prompt_ko: 70세에 처음 비행기를 타고 대서양을 건너는 엘리너
subtitle: Eleanor Parish had never been on an airplane. At seventy years old, she crossed the Atlantic for the first time — the same ocean that had separated her from Tom for half a century.

prompt: Sophie driving Eleanor through rural Pennsylvania autumn roads. Silence between them — some journeys don't need words. Then Eleanor standing at Tom's grave, carrying the pocket watch. She faces the headstone for a long time.
prompt_ko: 소피가 엘리너를 묘지로 데려가고 엘리너가 톰의 무덤 앞에 서는 장면
subtitle: Sophie drove her from the airport to the cemetery. They didn't speak much. Some journeys don't need words. Eleanor stood at Tom's grave for a long time. She was carrying the pocket watch. Sophie had given it to her the day they met.

prompt: Eleanor placing the brass pocket watch on Tom's headstone. She speaks to him — 'I'm here, Tom. I'm finally here.' She tells him about the letter and his terrible, beautiful handwriting. Sunset light on the grave. Sophie watches from a distance.
prompt_ko: 엘리너가 시계를 묘비에 올려놓고 톰에게 말하는 장면
subtitle: Eleanor placed the watch on the headstone. "I'm here, Tom. I'm finally here. I got your letter. It only took fifty years. And that last line — in your terrible, beautiful handwriting — that's the finest thing anyone has ever written to me."

prompt: Eleanor at the grave as the sun sets behind bare Pennsylvania trees. She laughs unexpectedly — a small clear bell-like laugh. She jokes about the kitchen he owed her. Joy breaking through grief. The most human moment.
prompt_ko: 석양 아래 웃으며 '부엌을 빚졌다'고 농담하는 엘리너
subtitle: She stood there until the sun began to set. Then she did something Sophie didn't expect. She laughed. A small, clear laugh, like a bell. "You owe me a kitchen, Thomas Bennett. A kitchen big enough to dance in."

prompt: Evening. Eleanor hands Sophie a yellowed sealed envelope that has never been opened. Eleanor explains she wrote it the same night Tom wrote his, June 5, 1944. She was going to give it to him when he came back.
prompt_ko: 엘리너가 1944년 같은 밤에 쓴 자신의 봉인된 편지를 소피에게 건네는 장면
subtitle: That evening, Eleanor handed Sophie a small envelope. Yellowed, sealed, never opened. "I wrote this on June 5th, 1944. The same night he wrote his. I was going to give it to him when he came back. He never came back. So I kept it."

prompt: Sophie carefully opening the fifty-year-old sealed envelope. Inside, Eleanor's neat handwriting. She reads: 'Come back to me, Thomas Bennett. I'll be waiting. I'll always be waiting.' Then Sophie places both letters side by side on the table — finally together.
prompt_ko: 소피가 엘리너의 편지를 열어 읽고 두 편지를 나란히 놓는 장면
subtitle: Sophie opened it carefully. "Come back to me, Thomas Bennett. I don't care if it takes a week or a year. I'll be waiting. I'll always be waiting." Sophie placed the two letters side by side. Tom's words, written by George's hand, finished by Tom's. Eleanor's words, sealed for fifty years.

prompt: Overhead shot of two letters lying side by side on a table — never sent, never received, yet somehow they have found each other. The pocket watch between them, still ticking. Warm golden light. A moment of perfect symmetry and closure.
prompt_ko: 두 편지가 나란히 놓인 모습 - 보내지 못한 두 편지가 마침내 만나다
subtitle: Two letters. Never sent. Never received. And yet — somehow — they had found each other.

prompt: Christmas scene: Eleanor sitting in Tom's old chair at the Bennett family home. She laughs at stories about Tom, tells the family things they never knew — he sang when alone, feared spiders, carried a wounded soldier two miles. Warm family gathering.
prompt_ko: 크리스마스에 베넷 가족과 함께하며 그들이 몰랐던 톰의 이야기를 들려주는 엘리너
subtitle: Eleanor spent that Christmas with us. She sat in my grandfather's chair and laughed at stories about him. She told us things we never knew — that he sang when he thought no one was listening, that he was afraid of spiders, that he once carried a wounded soldier two miles.

prompt: Eleanor giving the family back the man they thought they knew. Then spring: Eleanor's Cotswolds cottage. She sits peacefully in her armchair by the window. Watch in one hand, letter in the other, Tom's photo on the wall above. She has found peace.
prompt_ko: 엘리너의 평화로운 마지막 - 시계와 편지를 쥔 채 창가 의자에서
subtitle: She gave us back the man we thought we knew. And we gave her the ending she'd waited for. Eleanor passed away the following spring. Peacefully. In her cottage. They found her in the armchair by the window, the watch in one hand, Tom's letter in the other, his photograph on the wall.

prompt: Richard returning from Eleanor's funeral in England, carrying the pocket watch. He opens it for Sophie — inside are now two letters folded together. Finally together. Close-up of both letters nestled inside the watch mechanism.
prompt_ko: 리처드가 장례식에서 돌아와 시계 안에 함께 접힌 두 편지를 보여주는 장면
subtitle: My father flew to England for the funeral. When he came back, he was carrying the pocket watch. Inside it now were two letters. Folded together. Finally together.

prompt: Sophie holding the pocket watch with both letters inside. Slow contemplative shot as she reflects on love that endures. The two handwritings overlapping — his rough scrawl and her careful script. Time cannot kill real love.
prompt_ko: 사랑은 시간이나 거리나 침묵으로도 사라지지 않는다는 깨달음
subtitle: Some people say that love fades with time. That distance kills it. That silence buries it. But I held those two letters — his words and hers, written on the same night, kept for the same fifty years — and I knew that wasn't true.

prompt: Final shot: the pocket watch lying open on Tom's hand-built kitchen table, two letters visible inside. Warm golden light slowly fading. The watch still ticking. Love found its way. Fade to black.
prompt_ko: 마지막 장면 - 할아버지는 예쁜 글을 못 썼지만 사랑했고 편지는 길을 찾았다
subtitle: My grandfather couldn't write pretty words. But he loved her. And she waited. And in the end, the letter found its way.
